twitter.post: Sometimes a headline just isn't enough. When you need to understand a complex story, try our Deep Dive mode. It breaks things down with visuals and lets you ask follow-up questions to get the context you are looking for. HEADLINNE.com #News #Learning

linkedin.title: Most news feeds are designed to keep you scrolling, not to keep you informed.
linkedin.body:
We have all felt that frustration of opening a news app and seeing a wall of headlines that just do not matter to us. Algorithms usually prioritize whatever is loud or controversial because that is what gets clicks. But that approach misses the point of journalism.

Real personalization means something different. It means respecting your time by surfacing stories that actually align with your interests and professional needs. It is about cutting through the noise so you can get the information you need and get on with your day.

We built Headlinne because we believe technology should work for the reader, not the other way around. By using AI to understand what you care about, we can deliver a feed that feels like it was curated by a human editor who knows you personally.

I am curious to know how you currently manage your news consumption. Do you feel like you are getting what you need, or is it time for a better way to stay updated?
linkedin.cta: You can see how we are rethinking the news feed at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Philippine impeachment trial begins
slides.explanation: The Philippine Senate is officially opening an impeachment trial against the vice president. This process is expected to be politically intense as the country watches closely.

slides.headline: Florida Republican opposes Haiti deportations
slides.explanation: Congressman Carlos Giménez is speaking out against the plan to end temporary protected status for Haitians. He argues that deporting them right now would be a major mistake given the situation in Haiti.

slides.headline: AustralianSuper returns to coal investing
slides.explanation: Australia's largest superannuation fund is now the top investor in a major coal miner. This move is raising questions about the fund's previous promises to reach net zero emissions.

slides.headline: MLB player faces family crisis
slides.explanation: Los Angeles Dodgers catcher Eliézer Alfonzo just made his major league debut. He is currently playing while his sister and stepmother remain missing following earthquakes in Venezuela.

slides.headline: Iran prepares for leader's funeral
slides.explanation: Tehran is bracing for massive crowds as the funeral procession for the late supreme leader Ali Khamenei takes place today. Millions of people are expected to attend the event.

instagram.caption: From political shakeups in the Philippines to a major funeral procession in Iran, here is what is happening in the world today. We are tracking the top stories to keep you updated on the issues that matter. Check out the slides for the details. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Finance Stories Today

slides.headline: Macquarie likes Chinese AI chip stocks
slides.explanation: Analysts at Macquarie have started covering five Chinese companies that build AI chips. They think now is a smart time to look at these specific investments.

slides.headline: Wegovy pill arrives in UK pharmacies
slides.explanation: You can now buy a daily weight loss pill from the makers of Wegovy at private pharmacies in the UK. It is a new option for those looking for weight management solutions.

slides.headline: Beat the new EU airport queues
slides.explanation: New digital checks at EU borders might cause long waits at airports this summer. There are a few steps you can take now to make sure these delays do not cost you extra money.

slides.headline: Foxconn sees boost from AI demand
slides.explanation: Foxconn reported a jump in revenue for the second quarter thanks to the growing demand for AI technology. However, the company is still being cautious about global political tensions.

slides.headline: EasyJet eyes potential takeover deal
slides.explanation: EasyJet has reached an agreement in principle regarding a possible takeover. This follows the airline turning down four previous offers from the investment firm Castlelake.

instagram.caption: From new airport rules to big moves in the tech sector, here is your finance update for today. We have broken down the top business stories you need to know about. Swipe through for the latest headlines. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
